assertions.text: The vehicle drifted onto the shoulder (left or right shoulder unspecified).
The vehicle contacted the back (rear) of a parked trailer.
There was a single impact (one bump) with the parked trailer.
The collision was a light tap (minor severity).
The trailer was parked (stationary) at the time of contact.
The vehicle remained driveable after the impact.
The vehicle drifted onto the shoulder before making contact with the trailer.

verdicts.evidence: The telemetry shows steering input baseline [7.5](T3) deg and a peak raw steering [40.5](T3) deg at [t=0.0](T3), but device-frame steering angle and sparse pre-crash samples cannot determine a lateral drift onto the shoulder; this is therefore unverifiable from sensors alone.

The crash-pulse cumulative longitudinal delta-V reaches [‑98.6 km/h](T2) at [t=0.24 s](T3), indicating a large longitudinal impulse, but axis alignment to vehicle front/rear vs device frame is undocumented; telemetry does not explicitly record 'rear of trailer' contact direction, so this claim is unverifiable.

Detect_events found a single crash-pulse peak with cumulative longitudinal delta-V magnitude [98.6 km/h](T2) at [t=0.24 s](T2) and compute_stats reports delta_v_long final_value [‑98.2 km/h](T3); the telemetry shows one large impact pulse (one recorded delta-V event) rather than a small single bump — the assertion of a single mild bump is contradicted by the recorded single very large delta-V of [98.6 km/h](T2).

EDR summary and crash-pulse show a maximum longitudinal delta-V of [‑99.1 km/h](T1) (EDR summary) with detected peak [‑98.6 km/h](T2), which is far larger than a 'light tap' and indicates high-severity impact; thus 'light tap' is contradicted by the telemetry.

There is no separate telemetry for the trailer; sensors show vehicle speed steady between [98.0 km/h](T3) and [96.0 km/h](T3) in pre-crash samples and a large crash pulse [‑98.6 km/h](T2) at impact, but whether the struck object was stationary cannot be determined from V1 channels alone, so this is unverifiable.

The crash-pulse cumulative delta-V final value is [‑98.2 km/h](T3) with EDR max_delta_v_long_kmh [‑99.1 km/h](T1), indicating a very large impact; such a pulse is inconsistent with the vehicle remaining driveable and telemetry shows engine RPM/brake/throttle changes (compute_stats shows brake transitions and steering peak) — the claim vehicle remained driveable is contradicted by the severe recorded delta-V of [‑98.6 km/h](T2).

Pre-crash speed changes are small (first speed [98.0 km/h](T3) to last pre-impact [96.0 km/h](T3)), and steering peaks at [t=0.0](T3) with peak value [40.5 deg](T3), but the sparse sampling and undefined mapping between steering and lateral position mean the sequence 'drift onto shoulder before impact' cannot be determined from available channels, so this is unverifiable.

result.rationale: Telemetry shows a single very large longitudinal crash pulse (EDR max [‑99.1 km/h](T1); detected peak [‑98.6 km/h](T2); final cumulative [‑98.2 km/h](T3)) inconsistent with a minor 'light tap' and driveable outcome; several narrative details about drift, contact location, parked/stationary status, and sequence are unverifiable from the vehicle's sensors alone.

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

detections.criterion: crash-pulse cumulative delta-V at its maximum

result.thresholds_doc: impact spike: |a-baseline|>2.5g (>=20ms, merged<0.25s); sustained: 0.5s-avg in 0.35-1.2g; rotation: >40.0deg/s for 0.3s; net rotation >60.0deg; speed drop: >=15.0km/h in <=3.0s; brake: 0->1 transitions; CISS impact: max |crash-pulse delta-V|

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse
